commentary_text: The sportsbooks assign Belgium a 68% win probability versus the model's 38%, a 30-percentage-point gap favoring the favorite. This stark divergence suggests the market has access to information sharpening its conviction—recent form, lineup changes, or injury news could all push confidence in Belgium higher than the Elo-based forecast captures. The model sees Iran as a genuine contender at 31%, while the market prices them at just 12%, reflecting a similar confidence gap on the underdog side. The dashboard surfaces this disagreement as a signal for review rather than a judgment on which source has read the situation correctly.
